rulings.Case Title: CATALINA MARKETING CORP VS. COUPONS.COM. INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 17, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER CATALINA MARKETING CORP'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Granted. The Court will execute the proposed judgment submitted by Petitioner Catalina Marketing Corp. with the following modifications: (i) The first sentence of paragraph 2 at page 2, line 6 will read "Respondent shall implement the relief set forth on pages 56-57 of the Corrected Final Award and Exhibit A attached thereto."; (ii) The words "PERMANENT INJUNCTION" at line 10 of page 2 are deleted; (iii) The words "this Judgment" at line 18 on page 2 are replaced with "the Corrected Final Award"; (iv) the paragraph number headings at line 18 - 22 on page 2 are corrected to be 1 through 4; and (v) there will not be an exhibit to the Judgment. Respondent has not established that the recordkeeping procedure set forth in the proposed judgment as so stated is confidential. The parties will bear their own attorney fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion By Deft Marcus Daniel Merchasin In Support Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Pltff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Pltff Mario Galande And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 17, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT MARCUS MERCHASIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Plaintiff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Plaintiff Mario Galande And His Attorney. Defendant's motion is granted. Plaintiff Mario Galande shall serve further verified responses to all requests for production in which Plaintiff responded, "The documents requested are in possession of the Defendant and will be included in this production." Plaintiff shall amend the responses to state that all responsive, non-privileged documents in the custody, control or possession of the Plaintiff have been produced. If there are additional responsive, non-privileged documents in Plaintiff's custody or control that have not yet been produced, those documents shall be produced when the further responses are served. Plaintiff shall provide code-compliant, responsive, verified further responses to requests for production Nos. 16, 27, 28, and 37. Plaintiff shall provide a privilege log for all documents withheld from production related to request for production No. 52. Plaintiff's further responses and any additional documents required by this order shall be served on Defendant no later than November 27, 2014. Plaintiff opposed Defendant's motion without substantial justification and did not meet his obligations to meet and confer on these issues. Accordingly, Plaintiff Mario Galande and Plaintiff's counsel, Rey Hassan, are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $750, payable to Defendant no later than December 17, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLY CARPER et al VS. ADKNOWLEDGE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Attendance At Depositions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 17, 2014, Line 2, Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Attendance At Depositions And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is DENIED in its entirety. The motion to compel the plaintiffs' attendance at deposition is moot as the depositions took place. The plaintiffs' objections to the facially defective original deposition notices was validly served pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.410(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA WEIST-SANDIGO VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Or In The Alternative, Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 17, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC, EUROMOTORS, INC. DBA MERCEDES-BENZ OF SAN FRANCISCO, BERBERIAN BROS., INC. DBA BERBERIAN EUROPEAN MOTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Or In The Alternative, Summary Judgment. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants the motion as to the claim for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability as against MBUSA, which is an independent claim notwithstanding being included within the same enumerated cause of action as the express warranty claim. The claim is time barred. The cause of action accrued upon the date of tender (April 30, 2006), and plaintiff failed to file suit within 4 years thereafter. See Cal. Comm. Code sec. 2725. The Court denies the motion as to the breach of express warranty cause of action as against MBUSA. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding when the cause of action accrued and/or whether the statute of limitations was tolled or estoppel applies in light of contested issues concerning the content of and reasonable inferences to be drawn from communications in October of 2009. The remaining issues in this motion are denied as moot based on the dismissals filed on October 30, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 17, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Prior to the hearing, the parties are to meet and confer regarding a proposed order permitting the site inspection that would account for the least amount of disruption to operations and ensure the preservation of patient privacy. The parties are to bring the proposed order with them to the hearing and be prepared to argue the motion as well as any aspect of the proposed order. =(302/JPT)
